theme.title: Theme
theme.description: Customize the appearance for Godot Launcher

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: Choose Godot Launcher behavior for projects.
projects.confirmRemove: Ask for confirmation when removing projects.

autoStart.title: Startup Preference
autoStart.description: Choose what GD Launcher does when your computer starts
autoStart.linuxWarning: Auto start currently is not supported on Linux

projectLaunch.title: Project Launch Action
projectLaunch.description: Choose what GD Launcher does after launching a project

projectsLocation.title: Project Location
projectsLocation.description: Select a location for new projects. Existing projects will not be affected.

editorsLocation.title: Editor Installs Location
editorsLocation.description: Choose a location for Editor installs. Existing installs will not be affected.

clearReleaseCache.title: Refresh Release Cache
clearReleaseCache.description: Delete cached Godot release metadata and fetch the latest builds from GitHub.
clearReleaseCache.cooldownNotice: Cache refreshing is temporarily disabled to avoid hitting GitHub rate limits.
clearReleaseCache.successMessage: Release lists were refreshed with the latest builds.
clearReleaseCache.errorMessage: Something went wrong while refreshing the release cache. Try again later.

language.label: Language
language.description: Select language for Godot Launcher (does not change Godot language)

tools.overview: Launcher quickly validates cached tool paths when this tab gains focus. Run a full rescan if you changed installations on this system.

git.title: Git
git.description: Git is a distributed version control system for managing code changes and collaboration.

vscode.title: Visual Studio Code
vscode.description: A fast, extensible code editor with GDScript and .NET support for Godot.
vscode.notDetectedMessage: Godot Launcher is only able to automatically detect VS Code if it was installed in the PATH. You can instead specify the path to the vs code executable below
vscode.pathLabel: Visual Studio Code Path to Executable

updates.title: Updates
updates.description: Configure how GD Launcher checks for updates
updates.betaChannelDescription: Download prerelease builds from GitHub; these may be less stable than official releases.
updates.readyWithVersion: Version {{version}} is ready, restart Godot Launcher to install it. <Button>Restart now</Button>
updates.readyNoVersion: The update is ready, restart Godot Launcher to install it. <Button>Restart now</Button>

windowsSymlinks.title: Editor symlinks
windowsSymlinks.description: Choose whether project editors use optional symbolic links or stay with local copies.
windowsSymlinks.checkbox: Use symbolic links for Windows project editors. This stays off by default so you can opt in when you are ready for potential elevation prompts during editor updates.
windowsSymlinks.note: Changing this setting does not convert existing project links; it only affects how future editor refreshes behave.

enableDescription.line1: When enabled, Godot Launcher links each project to the installed editor instead of copying it.
enableDescription.line2: This keeps disk usage low, but Windows may request administrator approval when new symlinks are created.
enableDescription.line3: Existing projects keep their current local copies until their editor version is changed or reinstalled.

disableDescription.line1: Switch back to local copies to avoid elevation prompts. Godot Launcher will copy the editor files into each project instead.
disableDescription.line2: Existing symlinks remain in place until their editor version is changed or reinstalled.
